characters.name: Deidra "Dee" Mitchell
characters.description: Deidra MitchellBiographical informationAlias(es)DeeGenderFemaleBornJuly 9th, 1953 (age 70)Age43 (season 1)44 (season 2)45 (season 3)46 (season 4)47 (season 5)48 (season 6)ResidenceLos Angeles, CAOccupationVice PrincipalRelationsRelativesFrank Mitchell(second husband)Moesha Mitchell(stepdaughter)Myles Mitchell(stepson)Dorian Long(paternal stepson fromBarbara Lee)Bernie Mitchell(brother-in-law)Sandy Mitchell(sister-in-law)Charisse(niece)Carolyn Mitchell(sister-in-law) (unseen)Clark Mitchell(nephew-in-law)Roosevelt Mitchell(father-in-law)Arthur Moss(father)Shelby Moss(brother)Ruth Mitchell(mother-in-law)Love interest(s)Frank Mitchell(husband)Behind the scenesAppears inMoeshaFirst appearancePilotLast appearanceD-Money Loses His Patience (Season 5)Paying The Piper (recurring in Season 6)Portrayed bySheryl Lee Ralph Paying The Piper (recurring in Season 6) Deidra Moss "Dee" Mitchellis one of the main characters on the UPN seriesMoesha. She was portrayed by Sheryl Lee Ralph. The beautiful, beguiling, and glamorous Deidra Moss 'Dee' Mitchell was a teacher at her step-daughter, Moesha's school, Crenshaw High School. She became the faculty advisor for the school newspaper "Cry of the Cougar" and was later promoted to vice-principal. Even though Moesha didn't see eye to eye with her new stepmom, they had a close relationship. Later on in the series, Dee was offered a full-time education position in Jamaica, but she later returned for Dorian's high school graduation. The series was revamped and Dee was eventually phased out of the series for season 6 but she would appear as a recurring character in season 6. Dee returned back to the series from Jamaica for Dorian's high school graduation.

characters.description: Dorian LongBiographical informationAlias(es)D-MoneyGenderMaleBornFebruary 14, 1983 (Age 40)Age16 (turned 17) (Season 5)17 -18(Season 6)ResidenceLos Angeles, CaliforniaOccupationRapperRelationsRelativesFrank Mitchell(biological father/adoptive uncle)Barbara Lee (biological mother)Sandy Mitchell Long (adoptive mother/biological paternal aunt)Samuel Long (adoptive father/biological uncle-in-law)Bernie Mitchell (paternal uncle)Sandy Mitchell (paternal aunt)Roosevelt Mitchell (paternal grandfather)[[Deidra "Dee" Mitchell]] (Nèe. Moss Mitchell) (paternal stepmother)Marguerite Jennings-Mitchell† (paternal stepmother) (died)Moesha Mitchell(biological paternal half-sister/adoptive cousin)Myles Mitchell(biological paternal brother)FriendsWallace "Nice"Love interest(s)Lanae Foster (ex-girlfriend)Behind the scenesAppears inMoeshaFirst appearanceGood Virbrations?Last appearancePaying The PiperPortrayed byRay J (William Ray Norwood, Jr.) 17 -18(Season 6) Dorian Antonio Longis a character on the UPN seriesMoesha. He was portrayed by Ray J. Norwood. Dorian Long is the half brother of Moesha and Myles. He is introduced in season 5 as Frank's nephew. It is later revealed that Frank's sister Sandy adopted Dorian and Frank is his real father. His real mother is the women Frank had a secret affair with during his marriage to Moesha and Myles' mother. Near the end of Season 5, Dorian was involved with a gang, and committed theft for the ringleader, Patience. When Dorian gets caught with 5,000 dollars in his pocket, Frank and Dee cannot trust him, and send him to a Military camp. People can infer he is acting out because of Frank's secret affair with a mystery woman. After coming back from the Military camp in Season 6, he's been doing well. Trivia:Ray J was Charles Sanderson in Seasons 1-3, and guest starring as himself in Season 4.

characters.name: Frank Mitchell
characters.description:
Frank MitchellBiographical informationGenderMaleBornJanuary 15, 1954Age41-42 (season 1)42 (season 2)43 (season 3)44 (season 4)45 (season 5)46 (season 6)ResidenceLos Angeles, CaliforniaOccupationCar dealerRelationsRelativesRuth Mitchell(mother)Roosevelt Mitchell(father)Bernard "Bernie" Mitchell(younger brother)Sandy Mitchell-Long(sister)Carolyn Mitchell(sister)Marguerite Jennings-Mitchell† (first wife; deceased)Deidra "Dee" Mitchell(second wife)Moesha Denise Mitchell(daughter)Myles Mitchell(son)Dorian Long(son)Emerald Mitchell(son)Arthur Moss(father in-law)Shelby Moss(brother in-law)Clark Mitchell(nephew)Love interest(s)Barbara Lee(affair)Behind the scenesAppears inMoeshaFirst appearancePilotLast appearancePaying The PiperPortrayed byWilliam Allen YoungFrank Mitchellis one of the main characters on the UPN seriesMoesha. He was portrayed byWilliam Allen Young. Deidra "Dee" Mitchell(second wife)  Frank Mitchell owns a Saturn car dealership and the widowed father of Moesha and Myles Mitchell. He married his second wife/ Moesha and Myles' stepmother in 1995 named, Deidra Moss 'Dee' Mitchell (Married for 6 months as of S1. Ep.4). He used to work at a Saturn dealership before he bought it. It was later revealed by a close relative that he had an affair with another woman, Barbara Lee, (season 6, portrayed by Olivia Brown), during his first marriage to Marguerite Jennings Mitchell, (deceased, died 1992, R.I.P.) and had a child with her. 
The child Dorian Long, (portrayed by Brandy's real-life brother Ray J. Norwood) was raised by Frank's sister, Sandy. Dorian believed Sandy Mitchell was his real mother but it turned out that Barbara Lee was his real birth mother. The revelation of Frank's affair and paternity caused Dorian to rebel and Moesha to move out of the house permanently. He can sometimes be hypocritical in his views/convictions. For instance, when Dee doesn't disclose that Moesha was taking birth control pills, he felt betrayed believing he had a right to know what's going on in her life. Meanwhile, he brought Dorian to live with them without telling Dee that Dorian was his son. His relationship with Dorian is rocky despite having similar growing pains. For instance, Dorian got pulled over by the police after taking a car from Frank's lot to make a run for Patience (played by Master P). When Frank was younger, he told Dorian that he somehow allowed his younger brother, Bernie (played by Bernie Mac) to talk him into stealing a car; which led to him working in an auto shop changing tires.

characters.name: Hakeem Campbell
characters.description: Hakeem CampbellBiographical informationAlias(es)KemmieGenderMaleBornApril 29, 1980Age15-16 (season 1)17 (season 2)17 (season 3)17-18 (season 4)19-20 (season 5)21 (season 6)ResidenceLos Angeles, CaliforniaOccupationBusiness ManagementRelationsRelativesBernetta Campbell (mother)Calvin Campbell (father)Woody Campbell (cousin)Omar Campbell (couisn)Katherine Campbell (younger sister)FriendsMoesha MitchellKim ParkerNiecy JacksonQuinton 'Q' Brooks (former friend)Antonio Avalon (Season 4)Jerome (Season 6)Love interest(s)Kimberly Parker(ex-girlfriend)Moesha Mitchell(ex-girlfriend)Geneva(Season 1)Teresa(Season 2)Jennifer Sutton(Season 3)Tiffany Jesup(Season 5)Stevie Van Lowe(The Parkers)Behind the scenesAppears inMoeshaFirst appearancePilotLast appearancePaying The PiperRoad Trip (The Parkers)Portrayed byLamont Bentley 17 (season 2) 17 (season 3) Hakeem Campbell was born on April 29, 1980. He wasMoesha's special best friend and next-door neighbor. After finishing high school, he was accepted into Santa Monica College. When Moesha is offered a summer job touring as a business manager for her boyfriend, Q's rap group, Moesha turned it down to stay with Hakeem. He and Moesha started a relationship, but due to a misunderstanding, they broke up and Moesha went on tour with Q. After Moesha broke up with Q, she and Hakeem reconciled their relationship. He later datedKim Parker's best friendStevie Van Lowe. Hakeem is very friendly, good-natured, down to earth, very easy to talk to, and loves to eat, He's very loyal to the Mitchell's, always there for Moesha when she needs him, and really cares about her. Out of all Moesha's boyfriends, Hakeem is very romantic and he's the Prince Charming to Brandy's Cinderella.

(Season 4)At Last (The Parkers)Portrayed byCountess Danielle VaughnKimberly Ann "Kim" Parkeris one of the main characters on the UPN seriesMoeshaand the main protagonist of  its spin-offThe Parkers. She was portrayed by Countess Vaughn. The Parkers: At Last (The Parkers) Kim Parker was born on October 31, 1981, on Halloween in Los Angeles, California, she was Moesha Mitchell's best friend. They've known each other since preschool. She is funny, silly, and lovable, and is very caring, but very dimwitted. Even though she is usually behind most people when it comes to academics, her big heart and her dreams make up for it. 
Her mother, Nicole Ann 'Nikki' Parker gave birth to her when she was born in Los Angeles, California, and was still in high school at the time. After she completes and finishes high school, she is accepted into Santa Monica College, and then she was written out of the show left the series after Season 4, when her spinoff "The Parkers" began. She is surprised and excited when her mother, Nikki decides to go back to school, attending the same college as her daughter. Even when she goes to a different college than Moesha does, she manages to stay in touch with her.

characters.description: Marguerite Jennings-Mitchellwas the first wife ofFrank Mitchelland the biological mother ofMoeshaandMyles Mitchell. She passed on sometime when Moesha was younger and after Myles was born. She was only seen in flashbacks.

characters.description: MichaelBiographical informationGenderMaleBorn1981Died2001Age15-16 (Moesha)19 (The Parkers)OccupationBasketball teammate (former), Mechanic worker (job where he got accidentally killed)RelationsRelativesAlisa (Daughter)Unnamed GrandfatherFriendsKim Parker (romantic)Hakeem Campbell (neutral)Quinton 'Q' Brooks (friend)Moesha Mitchell (neutral)Niecy Jackson (neutral)Love interest(s)Kim Parker (former girlfriend)Yolanda (former partner/Alisa's mother)Behind the scenesAppears inMoeshaThe ParkersPortrayed byAntwon TannerMichael (portrayed by Antwon Tanner) wasKim’sformer boyfriend. First seen inMoesha, Michael was known as an acquaintance of Q's (Quinton Brooks). The two played basketball with each other for Crenshaw. His first interaction with Kim was around the time of Moesha's big dinner with her friends. Each of the girls brought dates. In Kim's attempt to grab Hakeem's attention, Michael hinted at wanting to be her date (to which Kim slightly brushed off). In later seasons, He can be seen still pursuing Kim. Eventually, they do get together at a party being hosted at The Den. While Kim initially went with Michael to try and make Hakeem jealous, Michael expresses that he is fully aware of her game. However, he hadn't cared much since he had feelings for Kim ever since they played the Dozens their freshman year.  Kim swoons, finally getting over her crush onHakeem. The two can be seen together ranging from seasons 1-3 of Moesha and at the end of season 2 ofThe Parkers. 19 (The Parkers) Unnamed Grandfather

characters.name: Myles Mitchell
characters.description: Myles MitchellBiographical informationAlias(es)Little Man (Seasons 5 & 6)GenderMaleBornMarch 13, 1988Age8 (season 1)9 (season 2)10 (season 3)11 (season 4)12 (season 5)13 (season 6)ResidenceLos Angeles, CARelationsRelativesFrank Mitchell(father)*Marguerite Jennings Mitchell† (deceased mother)*Deidra Moss 'Dee' Mitchell(stepmother)*Moesha Denise Mitchell(sister; engaged to Quinton Brooks)*Dorian Long Mitchell(paternal half brother fromBarbara Lee)*Bernard "Bernie" Mitchell (paternal uncle)*Sandy Mitchell Long (paternal aunt)*Roosevelt Mitchell (paternal grandfather)*Ruth Mitchell (paternal grandmother)FriendsSammieTony (Seasons 5 & 6)Behind the scenesAppears inMoeshaThe ParkersFirst appearancePilotLast appearancePaying The PiperPortrayed byMarcus T. Paulk 9 (season 2) 10 (season 3) Myles Mitchell was born in Los Angeles, California on March 13, 1988. He is the younger brother of Moesha Denise Mitchell and paternal younger half brother of Dorian Long Mitchell. Myles was always known for playing practical jokes during the early seasons of the show. When Moesha moved out of the house after a blowout with their father Frank, Myles coped with the dysfunction by smoking weed. While at therapy, Myles disclosed that he didn't feel like he was part of the family; mostly because he's always being sent to his room and that all of the attention was put on his sister. He confessed to her that he felt like she puts her friends before him making him feel less important. Myles was last seen in the final episode before he supposedly gotten kidnapped by Dorian’s producer friend. Myles would be later saved being kidnapped from Dorian's producer friend as he is seen later inThe Parkers.

characters.description: Nicole Parker OgleveeBiographical informationGenderFemaleBornApril 24, 1962Age35 (Moesha appearances)The Parkers:36/37 (season 1)37/38 (season 2)38/39 (season 3)39/40 (season 4)40/41 (season 5)ResidenceSanta Monica, CaliforniaOccupationCaterer, Student at Santa Monica Community CollegeRelationsRelativesStanley Oglevee(husband)Kim Parker(daughter)Mel Parker(ex-husband)George West(adoptive father)Shirley West(adoptive mother)Rita DeJohn(biological mother)Quincy DeJohn(biological father)Constance McFarland(sister)Richard McFarland(brother in-law)Tiffany McFarland(niece)Willie Alexander(great-uncle)FriendsDesiree "Des" Littlejohn (season 1)Andell Wilkerson (best friend)Deidre Mitchell (friend)Sophia Van Lowe (friend)Stevie Van Lowe (friend)Love interest(s)Stanley Oglevee (crush-unrequited/husband)Behind the scenesAppears inMoeshaThe ParkersLast appearance"At Last"Portrayed byMo'niqueNicole "Nikki" Ann Parker Oglevee(née Alexander) is a character fromMoeshaand one of the 2main protagonists inThe Parkers. The Parkers:36/37 (season 1) 37/38 (season 2) Nikki first appeared on Moesha in an episode where she went back to school to get the last credit she needs. She had got pregnant at a young age which lead her to drop out of high school, get married to Kim's father (they later divorced), and put her dreams of going to college aside until Kim was 18. In her two appearances on Moesha Nikki goes with her daughter Kim to school eventually passing her classes and is able to graduate. She later decides to attend Santa Monica College with Kim after graduation. Their life at Santa Monica was followed in the spinoff show "The Parkers". In the first episode, Grape Nuts, she and her daughter want to try out to be in a sorority. It was later revealed that she was adopted in the episode, "Family Ties and Lies", where she and her family appear on a game show. She is also known to have a massive crush on Professor Stanley Oglevee. She has gone through his stuff and has chased away his many girlfriends, including his on/off girlfriend Veronica Cooper. After 5 years of chasing him, she finally marries him in the episode, "At Last".

characters.name: Professor Thornhill
characters.description: ThornhillBiographical informationAlias(es)Professor T.(Freddy, presumably by students)GenderFemaleOccupationPsychology professorBehind the scenesAppears inThe ParkersFirst appearance"Cheaters Never Prosper"(only appearance)Portrayed byRosalind Chao Professor Thornhillis a character fromThe Parkers. She is a psychology professor who teaches at Santa Monica College. Following the psychology department's study on various personality types, Professor Thornhill was given the opportunity to conduct her own experiment on her students. She explained that she wanted to put five students in a room together for seventy-two hours to evaluate them. Initially given a poor response from her students, she revealed that the winners get an even split of five thousand dollars, prompting every student to participate.
